raw_question_text:
Let \( x(t) \) represent a periodic signal with period \( T = 8 \) seconds, whose Fourier series coefficients are
\[
a_k = \begin{cases} 
\frac{1}{j \pi k} & \text{for integers } k \neq 0 \\
0 & k = 0
\end{cases}
\]
When \( x(t) \) is the input to a linear, time-invariant system with system function \( H(s) \) is shown in <image> (a),
the output \( y(t) \) is the sum of a constant \( \beta \) plus a periodic train of impulses with area 1,
\[
y(t) = \beta + \sum_{k=-\infty}^{\infty} \delta(t - 8k)
\]
as shown in the <image> (b).
Consider the output of the same system if the period of the input signal is changed to T = 4 seconds while keeping the Fourier series coefficients ak
unchanged.
Is it possible to determine the new output signal from the information provided?
extraction_notes: image field is a PIL.Image placeholder, not a path or URL
source_intake_agent

raw_question_text: Which of the following statements about estimates for \( g_m \) and \( r_0 \) is correct?
raw_answer_text: \( g_m \approx g_{m1} \) and \( r_0 \approx r_{01} \cdot g_{m2} \cdot r_{02} \)

choice_map.A: \( g_m \approx g_{m1} \cdot g_{m2} \cdot r_{02} \) and \( r_0 \approx r_{01} + r_{02} \)
choice_map.B: \( g_m \approx g_{m1} + g_{m2} \) and \( r_0 \approx r_{01} + r_{02} \)
choice_map.C: \( g_m \approx g_{m1} \) and \( r_0 \approx r_{01} \cdot g_{m2} \cdot r_{02} \)

raw_question_text: Two identical NMOS transistors M1 and M2 are connected as shown below. \(V_{bias}\) is chosen so that both transistors are in saturation. The equivalent \(g_m\) of the pair is:

choice_map.A: The sum of individual \(g_m\)'s of the transistors
choice_map.B: The product of individual \(g_m\)'s of the transistors
choice_map.C: Nearly equal to the \(g_m\) of M1

raw_question_text:
The state space equation of a system is described by 
\[
\dot{x} = Ax + Bu, \quad y = Cx
\]
where \( x \) is the state vector, \( u \) is the input, \( y \) is the output, and 
\[
A = \begin{bmatrix} 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 \end{bmatrix}, \quad B = \begin{bmatrix} 0 \\ 1 \end{bmatrix}, \quad C = [1 \quad 0]
\]

A unity feedback is provided to the above system \( G(s) \) to make it a closed-loop system as shown in the <image>. 

For a unit step input \( r(t) \), the steady-state error in the input will be ______.
